scenario: A team's Web Modeler project has a custom Connector Template uploaded. Modelers in this project can use it on Service Tasks. Other teams in the organisation also want access -- but only members of the original team are seeing it.
question: Is the Template scope project-specific or org-wide?

options.text: Templates automatically become available to all organisation members as soon as they are uploaded to any project -- no additional action is required, and the template appears in every project's template selector immediately. This claim is inaccurate. By default, when a team uploads a Connector Template to a Web Modeler project, it is scoped to that project only. Org-wide availability requires an explicit publishing step performed by a user with elevated access (organisation owner or administrator). The default is project-scoped, not org-wide.

Project specific by default -- Connector Templates uploaded to a project are visible only within that project. To make them available organisation wide, publish at organisation scope (where supported) so all projects can use them. Templates support a hierarchy of scopes; verify the current Web Modeler version's exact options.

Templates uploaded to a project are automatically available to all other projects owned by the same user who uploaded them -- the template follows the uploader's identity rather than the project's scope. This claim is also incorrect. Templates are scoped to the specific project they were uploaded to, regardless of who uploaded them. The template's default visibility is determined by its project location, not by the uploader's identity. Other projects (including the uploader's own other projects) would not automatically see the template.

Templates in Web Modeler are isolated per project and cannot be shared with other teams or other projects -- the only way for another team to use the template is to re-upload it to their own project manually. This claim is inaccurate. Web Modeler supports org-level template publishing: an organization owner or administrator can publish a template to organisation scope, making it available to all members and all projects within the organisation. This is documented in the element templates publishing guide and enables centralised governance of approved Connector Templates.

a.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler's template scoping defaults to the project where the template was uploaded. Per manage-element-templates.md:53 the default action button is Publish to project ("activate it within the project context"). For org-wide availability, a user with elevated access (per :71) uses Publish > Publish to organization on the editor screen or promotes a template version via the versions list -- "enabling all organization members to use it in their diagrams" (:71). Org members without elevated access can still view all org-published resources from the Web Modeler home page (:81-94). This two-tier scoping lets teams develop custom Connectors privately while still sharing approved ones across the org under governance control.

c.text: Incorrect. Not "uploader's projects" -- specifically the project where uploaded.
